{"problem_standardized_math":"Let $f$ be defined by\n\\[\nf(x)=h(x)+\\phi(x).\n\\]\nDefine the approximate gradient $\\nabla_{\\epsilon} f(x)$ by the forward-difference formula\n\\[\n\\nabla_{\\epsilon} f(x)=\\left[\\frac{f(x+\\epsilon e_i)-f(x)}{\\epsilon}\\right]_{i=1,2,\\ldots,n},\n\\]\ninstead of the central-difference formula. Prove the following variant of Lemma 9.1: If $\\nabla h(x)$ is Lipschitz continuous in a neighborhood of the box\n\\[\n\\{z\\mid z\\ge x,\\ \\|z-x\\|_{\\infty}\\le \\epsilon\\}\n\\]\nwith Lipschitz constant $L_h$, then\n\\[\n\\|\\nabla_{\\epsilon} f(x)-\\nabla h(x)\\|_{\\infty}\\le L_h\\epsilon+\\frac{\\eta(x;\\epsilon)}{\\epsilon},\n\\]\nwhere\n\\[\n\\eta(x;\\epsilon)=\\sup_{z\\ge x,\\ \\|z-x\\|_{\\infty}\\le \\epsilon}|\\phi(z)|.\n\\]"}
